2022-12-01.title: Special Court Needed for Russian Crimes in Ukraine, E.U. Chief Says
2022-12-01.abstract: The idea reflects frustration with the difficulty in holding anyone accountable in the war, and actually reaching trials in a new court is likely to be very hard.

2022-12-01.title: An Agonizing Wait for a Migrant Worker’s Final Journey Home
2022-12-01.abstract: The inequality and vulnerability that laborers from places like Nepal face overseas also stalk them in death, when dreams evaporate.

2022-12-01.title: After Xi’s Coronation, a Roar of Discontent Against His Hard-Line Politics
2022-12-01.abstract: Protests in China have roused a tradition of dissent that had seemed spent after 10 years under Xi Jinping. The effects may far outlast the street clashes.

2022-12-01.title: U.S. and Ukrainian Embassies Targeted by Letter Bombs in Spain
2022-12-01.abstract: One of the six packages containing “pyrotechnic material” was also sent to the prime minister’s office in Madrid, prompting the authorities to increase security.

2022-12-01.title: After Mass Protests, China Appears to Back Away From Harsh Covid Rules
2022-12-01.abstract: Several cities announced the easing of lockdown regulations and testing requirements after the country’s biggest demonstrations in decades.

2022-12-02.title: Brace for Bombs, Fix and Repeat: Ukraine’s Grim Efforts to Restore Power
2022-12-02.abstract: Even as Ukrainian workers race to restore basic services like electricity, heat and water, new Russian airstrikes send them back to the starting line.

2022-12-02.title: Prosecutor Drops Australia Parliament Rape Case, Citing Toll on Accuser
2022-12-02.abstract: Mental trauma that Brittany Higgins faced from the proceedings presented a “significant and unacceptable risk” to her life, the prosecutor said.

2022-12-02.title: Germans Have Seen the Future, and It’s a Heat Pump
2022-12-02.abstract: The boxy machines look and function like large air-conditioners on reverse, but Germans hope they hold the key to Europe’s push for fossil-free heating.

2022-12-02.title: Shadowy Arm of a German State Helped Russia Finish Nord Stream 2
2022-12-02.abstract: The threat of U.S. sanctions jeopardized completion of a second direct gas pipeline from Russia. So Gazprom and German officials concocted a phony climate foundation to get the job done.

2022-12-02.title: The Drummer Who Reminds People That ‘You Can Play Music as You Like’
2022-12-02.abstract: Miguel Tomasín, one of the few professional musicians with Down syndrome, has brought attention to the artistic visions of people with developmental disabilities, with his band releasing over 100 albums.

2022-12-03.title: Zelensky Proposes Barring Orthodox Church That Answers to Moscow
2022-12-03.abstract: Ukraine’s president called for legislation to prevent an ancient branch of the Orthodox Church, led by a Putin ally, from operating in his country, but it remains unclear how that would work.

2022-12-03.title: As Macron Loses His Sheen at Home, Harmonious U.S. Visit Is ‘Regenerative’
2022-12-03.abstract: President Emmanuel Macron, dealing with a difficult start to his second term, can return to France feeling buoyed by a warm reception and unity on Ukraine.

2022-12-03.title: He Returned a Dazed Soldier to the Russians. Ukraine Calls It Treason.
2022-12-03.abstract: No one knew what to do with a lost Russian pilot who suddenly appeared in the occupied city of Kherson. The case has revealed the blurred line between pragmatism in a war zone and collaboration with the enemy.

2022-12-03.title: Landslide Tragedy Turns Italy’s Focus to Illegal Construction
2022-12-03.abstract: After 11 deaths, a series of government amnesties for illegally built homes is becoming a focus of a blame game.

2022-12-03.title: After Half a Century, Prince Edward Island’s Musical Tradition Takes a Break
2022-12-03.abstract: The musical theater version of “Anne of Green Gables,” a favorite of tourists from around the world, will now be staged only every other year.

2022-12-04.title: The Kremlin’s defiance adds to questions over whether the price cap will work.
In Forests Full of Mines, Ukrainians Find Mushrooms and Resilience
2022-12-04.abstract: These misty and damp parts of the country have long beckoned to mushroom hunters with the promise of plenty, but now peril, too, lies beneath the earth’s surface.

2022-12-04.title: Arab Fans Confront Israeli Reporters Covering World Cup in Qatar
2022-12-04.abstract: When Qatar granted access to Israeli soccer fans and media despite an absence of diplomatic ties, some saw it as another sign of warming ties with the Arab world. Instead, some of the Israelis got a chilly reception.

2022-12-04.title: The Chinese Dream, Denied
2022-12-04.abstract: The world’s harshest Covid restrictions exemplify how Xi Jinping’s authoritarian excesses have rewritten Beijing’s longstanding social contract with its people.

2022-12-04.title: Abuse in K-Pop in Spotlight Again After L.A. Hotel Altercation
2022-12-04.abstract: A public dispute between band members and the head of their agency has revived concerns about whether South Korean entertainment agencies exploit young musicians.

2022-12-05.title: War and Sanctions Threaten to Thrust Russia’s Economy Back in Time
2022-12-05.abstract: While Russia’s economy has not collapsed, an exodus of Western companies is eroding hard-won progress, and experts say the worst may be yet to come.

2022-12-05.title: An E.U. embargo of Russian oil and the G7’s price cap take effect.
2022-12-05.abstract: An immediate impact on oil supplies in Europe was not expected, partly because the embargo has been in the works for months.

2022-12-05.title: Her Baby Needs Heart Surgery. But She Is Demanding ‘Unvaccinated’ Blood.
2022-12-05.abstract: New Zealand’s health service is seeking to take temporary custody of a critically ill infant so it can perform a lifesaving operation.

2022-12-05.title: Skyrocketing Prices in Turkey Hurt Families and Tarnish Erdogan
2022-12-05.abstract: Annual inflation is around its highest level in decades, devaluing salaries and devouring savings less than a year before President Recep Tayyip Erdogan runs for re-election.

2022-12-05.title: Covid Protests in China Raise Hope for Solidarity Among Activists Abroad
2022-12-05.abstract: People opposing Beijing over issues like Hong Kong, Taiwan or the persecution of Uyghurs see the moment as an opportunity to find common cause.

2022-12-06.title: Ukraine Targets Bases Deep in Russia, Showing Expanded Reach
2022-12-06.abstract: Launching drones at air bases 300 miles from its own territory, Ukraine changed the geography of the war. It said it had developed drones with a range of over 600 miles.

2022-12-06.title: Latvia Hosted Russian Journalists in Exile. Then Their Loyalties Were Questioned.
2022-12-06.abstract: A news host’s comment about helping troops prompted the Latvian authorities to revoke the license of TV Rain, a major independent outlet, putting a focus on the public role of Russians who fled.

2022-12-06.title: In Sweeping Legal Overhaul, Indonesia Outlaws Sex Outside Marriage
2022-12-06.abstract: Parliament also approved a law that criminalizes criticism of the government, delivering a blow to the country’s progressive reputation.

2022-12-06.title: Russia continues to manufacture cruise missiles despite Western sanctions, experts say.
2022-12-06.abstract: Weapons investigators in Kyiv found that at least one Russian Kh-101 cruise missile used in widespread attacks there on Nov. 23 had been made no earlier than October.

2022-12-06.title: Putin signs a law banning expressions of L.G.B.T.Q. identity in Russia.
2022-12-06.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia intensified his crackdown on L.G.B.T.Q. people on Monday, when he signed new legislation that widely bans public expression of their identity in the country.

2022-12-07.title: Ukraine Strikes More Boldly, Seeing Little Room for Russia to Escalate
2022-12-07.abstract: The third apparent drone attack in two days on a Russian air base indicates a new phase of the war for Ukraine as it develops longer-range weapons.

2022-12-07.title: Corrections: Dec. 7, 2022
2022-12-07.abstract: Corrections that appeared in print on Wednesday, Dec. 7, 2022.

2022-12-07.title: Russian Anti-War Activists Seek Common Goals (When They’re Not Bickering)
2022-12-07.abstract: Almost 300 activists, many young and from the diaspora, gathered to try to forge a common path, beyond struggling against Vladimir Putin and the war in Ukraine.

2022-12-07.title: China Eases ‘Zero Covid’ Restrictions in Victory for Protesters
2022-12-07.abstract: Beijing’s costly policy of lockdowns has pummeled the world’s second-largest economy and set off mass public protests that were a rare challenge to China’s leader, Xi Jinping.

2022-12-07.title: El Salvador’s Leader Has Eroded Rights to Tackle Violence. Is It Working?
2022-12-07.abstract: The president declared a state of emergency eight months ago to take on gang violence, and arrests soared. Close to 100,000 people are behind bars as of November, more than triple the prison system’s capacity.

2022-12-08.title: Putin Warns Russians to Prepare for Protracted Ukraine War
2022-12-08.abstract: Vladimir Putin appeared to acknowledge that subduing Russia’s neighbor was taking longer than expected, but said there was no need for another draft now.

2022-12-08.title: A Gray Area of Loyalties Splinters a Liberated Ukrainian Town
2022-12-08.abstract: “Friends are not friends anymore,’’ said one woman in the town of Sviatohirsk, which remains divided not by trench lines and artillery, but by where people’s allegiances lie: with Moscow or Kyiv.

2022-12-08.title: A Surge in Tourists in Restive Kashmir, but ‘No Mental Peace’ for Residents
2022-12-08.abstract: Visitors have flocked back to the region — proof, India says, that its imposition of control worked. But people who live there say fear and uncertainty persist.

2022-12-08.title: ‘Ebola Could Have Wiped Us All’: Slow Lockdown Haunts Uganda
2022-12-08.abstract: The authorities hesitated to impose restrictions in the epicenter of the Ebola outbreak because of residual trauma from the Covid lockdown. Ebola then spread to the capital, killing dozens of Ugandans.

2022-12-08.title: ‘Zero Covid,’ Once Ubiquitous, Vanishes in China’s Messy Pivot
2022-12-08.abstract: As China casts aside many Covid rules, it is also playing down the threat of the virus. The move could help ease the burden on hospitals but comes with its own risks.

2022-12-09.title: Brittney Griner Is Being Taken to an Army Hospital in San Antonio
2022-12-09.abstract: The hospital treats both civilians and military personnel who have survived torture or other trauma.

2022-12-09.title: North Korea Wants Dollars. It’s a Sign of Trouble.
2022-12-09.abstract: Kim Jong-un is desperately trying to find hard currency to fund his military and economic ambitions.

2022-12-09.title: New Zealand on a Plate
2022-12-09.abstract: Why Aotearoa is spectacularly situated to offer some of the world’s most interesting, varied, delicious food.

2022-12-09.title: Chasing the U.S., China’s Leader Emerges From Diplomatic Isolation
2022-12-09.abstract: Xi Jinping’s visit to Saudi Arabia highlights Beijing’s renewed bid to compete with Washington after years of preoccupation with the pandemic.

2022-12-09.title: Beijing Braces for Covid Surge After China Lifts Pandemic Curbs
2022-12-09.abstract: Residents lined up at hospitals and pharmacies, seeking help for fevers and buying at-home tests after the government’s decision to ease its “zero Covid” policy.

2022-12-10.title: Russia and Iran are strengthening their defense partnership, the Biden administration warns.
2022-12-10.abstract: The National Security Council spokesman, John Kirby, said that the U.S. was “concerned that Russia intends to provide Iran with advanced military components.”

2022-12-10.title: A Chatty Putin’s Underlying Message: I’m Still in Charge
2022-12-10.abstract: In a string of appearances, it wasn’t so much what the Russian leader said, much of it false, as it was his apparent need to demonstrate that he is healthy, alert and in control.

2022-12-10.title: Spat Over Patriot Missiles Reveals Deepening Rifts in Europe Over Ukraine
2022-12-10.abstract: Relations between Warsaw and Berlin, never warm, have deteriorated since the start of the Ukraine war, damaging unity in both NATO and the European Union.

2022-12-10.title: China’s Looming ‘Tsunami’ of Covid Cases Will Test Its Hospitals
2022-12-10.abstract: To conserve resources for the severely ill, the government is urging residents not to seek help unless necessary. But the pivot has left many confused and anxious.

2022-12-10.title: Brittney Griner Swap Puts Spotlight on Americans in Russia
2022-12-10.abstract: Westerners in Russia have to weigh the risks of living and working in the country against professional and financial opportunities there.

2022-12-11.title: War Next Door Brings Energy Crunch, and Paid Protests, to Moldova
2022-12-11.abstract: The tiny country, starved of natural gas and electricity because of the conflict in neighboring Ukraine, is confronting street rallies bankrolled by a pro-Russian politician to target its pro-Western government.

2022-12-11.title: Confidential Records Show a Saudi Golf Tour Built on Far-Fetched Assumptions
2022-12-11.abstract: McKinsey documents suggest the Saudi league is far off-track for success. Experts say the analysis shows it was never just about profits.

2022-12-11.title: The Prince, the Plot and a Long-Lost Reich
2022-12-11.abstract: Prince Heinrich XIII was arrested last week as the suspected ringleader of a plan to overthrow the German government. Nostalgic for an imperial past, he embraced far-right conspiracy theories.

2022-12-11.title: 4 Charged With Corruption in Bribery Inquiry Linked to Qatar
2022-12-11.abstract: The Belgian authorities announced other charges, including money laundering, in a case involving current and former members of the European Parliament.

2022-12-11.title: In Hostage Diplomacy, It’s Often the Hostage-Takers Who Pay
2022-12-11.abstract: Detaining foreigners to wring concessions from their home country’s government holds perils for both sides, but especially, perhaps surprisingly, for the hostage takers.

2022-12-12.title: Biden Is Bringing Africa’s Leaders to Washington, Hoping to Impress
2022-12-12.abstract: China, Russia, Turkey and the United Arab Emirates are all vying for influence in Africa. What will the Biden administration offer at a summit that starts Tuesday in Washington?

2022-12-12.title: What Happens to the Migrant Workers Who Built the World Cup?
2022-12-12.abstract: The World Cup was a $220 billion construction project powered by migrant workers. We went to Nepal — a major source of labor for Qatar — to find out what’s driving workers to do dangerous jobs that many say they can’t afford to lose.

2022-12-12.title: Russia Is Using Old Ukrainian Missiles Against Ukraine, General Says
2022-12-12.abstract: The missiles, returned to Russia in the 1990s under an agreement aimed at assuring Ukraine’s sovereignty and territorial integrity, have shown up in rubble.

2022-12-12.title: Snow and Cold Bring Parts of U.K. to a Standstill
2022-12-12.abstract: Freezing temperatures raised concerns about energy costs. Three children died after falling into an icy lake, the police said.

2022-12-12.title: Ukraine says it struck a hotel where members of Russia’s Wagner security force had gathered.
2022-12-12.abstract: The claim could not be independently verified, and there was no immediate comment or confirmation from the Wagner Group.

2022-12-13.title: Putin Skips Annual News Conference, Avoiding Possible Questions on War
2022-12-13.abstract: The December event has offered reporters rare — albeit stage-managed — chances to pose hard questions. With the war going poorly for Russia, that could have been awkward.

2022-12-13.title: 6 Dead, Including 2 Officers, After Shootout in Rural Australia
2022-12-13.abstract: The officers and a bystander were gunned down outside a property in Queensland, and the assailants were later killed by the police, officials said.

2022-12-13.title: ‘There Must Be More Room for Africa,’ Leader of African Union Says
2022-12-13.abstract: President Macky Sall of Senegal, chairman of the bloc, said in an interview before the U.S.-Africa summit that it’s time for the continent to have more seats in global institutions.

2022-12-13.title: Eldest BTS Member, Jin, Enlists in South Korean Army
2022-12-13.abstract: Saying goodbye to the ARMY — as the K-pop supergroup’s fan base is known — and hello to the armed forces, Jin became the first member of the boy band to start mandatory military service.

2022-12-13.title: As Covid Spreads Fast, Beijing Isn’t in Lockdown. But It Feels Like It.
2022-12-13.abstract: Almost no one is venturing out even after an easing of pandemic measures. Cold and flu medicines are scarce, but food supplies appear adequate.

2022-12-14.title: Joy and Anxiety as Moroccans Look to World Cup Match With France
2022-12-14.abstract: Morocco’s magical run has elated fans, but for some, the strain of the impending showdown with the country’s former colonial overlords is too much.

2022-12-14.title: Plan for More Donors and Faster Aid Yields $1 Billion for Ukraine Repairs
2022-12-14.abstract: A French-led effort to involve dozens of countries aims to help Ukraine survive a harsh winter with quick repair of vital electrical, heating, water and health care systems.

2022-12-14.title: Two Dogs From North Korea Become Victims of a Political Squabble
2022-12-14.abstract: Kim Jong-un, the North Korean leader, gave two pups to South Korea as a symbol of peace. Now the canines are at the center of a political spat between leaders in the South.

2022-12-14.title: At Least 4 Dead as Boat Capsizes in English Channel
2022-12-14.abstract: British and French maritime authorities were carrying out a major rescue operation, a day after Prime Minister Rishi Sunak of Britain announced new plans to slow such crossings.

2022-12-14.title: Defenses Carved Into the Earth
2022-12-14.abstract: Russia is building a vast network of trenches, traps and obstacles to slow Ukraine’s momentum. Will it work?

2022-12-15.title: The Infrared Hunt for Russian Troops in the Battle for Bakhmut
2022-12-15.abstract: On a frigid December night, The New York Times accompanied members of a surveillance team for the Ukrainian Army as they used a thermal sight to find enemy positions miles away.

2022-12-15.title: Nurses in U.K. Strike for First Time, Seeking Higher Pay
2022-12-15.abstract: The walkout is just one of a series of industrial actions across Britain this month as ballooning inflation, rising interest rates and a recession put pressure on workers.

2022-12-15.title: Falconry is an Ancient Sport. In Qatar, Drones Give it a Twist.
2022-12-15.abstract: The training regimen now involves drones, but doomed pigeons are still in the mix. Generations of Qataris have taken up the hobby.

2022-12-15.title: The U.S. imposes sanctions on a Russian oligarch and other officials.
2022-12-15.abstract: The sanctions block financial transactions in dollars with Vladimir Potanin, the largest shareholder in Nornickel, the world’s biggest producer of palladium and refined nickel.

2022-12-15.title: Boris Becker Returns to Germany After Release From British Prison
2022-12-15.abstract: The tennis champion left for his home country after serving time in Britain for hiding his assets in a bankruptcy case.

2022-12-16.title: Protesters in Peru Demand Justice for Ousted President Pedro Castillo
2022-12-16.abstract: Eight days after Pedro Castillo’s removal from office and arrest, thousands of his supporters have joined protests demanding his reinstatement. To them, he is the voice of the marginalized.

2022-12-16.title: Biden Courts African Leaders, but Some Are Skeptical of Big Promises
2022-12-16.abstract: President Biden faced a challenge in winning the confidence of a continent where the circumstances of its leaders vary so dramatically.

2022-12-16.title: N​orth Korea Says It Has Tested a New ICBM Engine
2022-12-16.abstract: ​The statement, issued Friday, followed an order from the country’s leader, Kim Jong-un, to develop a new ICBM that is harder to intercept.

2022-12-16.title: Fire in France Kills at Least 10, Including 5 Children
2022-12-16.abstract: Firefighters managed to put out the blaze, in a seven-floor building near Lyon, in central France. It was not immediately clear how the fire had started.

2022-12-16.title: Missiles knock out heating systems in towns and cities amid freezing temperatures.
2022-12-16.abstract: It was the ninth large-scale wave of missiles to be aimed at Ukrainian infrastructure this fall. Officials said 76 missiles had been fired, and all but 16 were shot down.

2022-12-17.title: Putin’s War: The Inside Story of a Catastrophe
2022-12-17.abstract: Secret battle plans, intercepted communications and Russian soldiers explain how a “walk in the park” became a catastrophe for Russia.

2022-12-17.title: Tunisia Heads for First Elections Since Presidential Power Grab
2022-12-17.abstract: Voters will choose a new Parliament, but under revised rules that vastly dilute the influence of political parties that many blame for sabotaging the North African nation’s 10-year experiment with democracy.

2022-12-17.title: Once a Figurehead of Change, Ireland’s Returning Leader Has Lots to Prove
2022-12-17.abstract: In Leo Varadkar’s first stint as prime minister, he embodied for many his country’s move into modernity. But after several missteps, he now takes up the role for a second time, with the glow of optimism dimmed.

2022-12-17.title: Eight Takeaways From The Times’s Investigation Into Putin’s War
2022-12-17.abstract: Secret Russian battle plans, intercepts, and interviews with Russian soldiers and Kremlin confidants revealed new details of how Vladimir Putin botched his invasion of Ukraine.

2022-12-17.title: How a Town Famous for Xenophobia Fell in Love With Immigrants
2022-12-17.abstract: “We now want as many immigrants as possible,” said Bernard Thompson, the mayor of Hérouxville, Quebec, a onetime supporter of the town’s anti-immigrant code.

2022-12-18.title: Famed Iranian Actress Arrested After Voicing Support for Protests
2022-12-18.abstract: The actress, Taraneh Alidoosti, had urged people to speak out after the execution of a man involved in nationwide demonstrations.

2022-12-18.title: Where Romantic Poetry in a Fading Language Draws Stadium Crowds
2022-12-18.abstract: That 300,000 people celebrated Urdu verse during a three-day festival was testament to the peculiar reality of the language in India.

2022-12-18.title: South Korea Accuses the North of Launching Missiles. The North Says It Was a Satellite Test.
2022-12-18.abstract: The North has made similar claims before when it was in fact preparing for ICBM launches.

2022-12-18.title: How Can Tainted Spinach Cause Hallucinations?
2022-12-18.abstract: A food recall from Australia sheds light on an unusual aspect of brain chemistry.

2022-12-18.title: Counting Russia’s War Dead, With Tips, Clips and a Giant Spreadsheet
2022-12-18.abstract: Amid doubts about Moscow’s true war losses, Russian data journalists and volunteers use news articles, tombstone photographs and other open-source information to tally the Russian toll from the war in Ukraine.

2022-12-19.title: In Heart of Peru’s Protest, a Pause to Mourn the Dead
2022-12-19.abstract: Confrontations between protesters and the Peruvian authorities have left at least 25 dead and hundreds injured. Nowhere may tensions be higher than in the highland city of Ayacucho.

2022-12-19.title: The Year in Pictures 2022
2022-12-19.abstract: Photographers captured historic moments of war, grief and wonder that defined the year.

2022-12-19.title: Overnight drone attacks target Kyiv and two other regions of Ukraine.
2022-12-19.abstract: Ukraine said that it had shot down 20 of 35 drones that Russia had launched, though those that evaded air defenses had hit power plants, electrical systems and other civilian targets.

2022-12-19.title: 5 Killed in Shooting at Canadian Condo Building, Police Say
2022-12-19.abstract: The 73-year-old gunman, who was fatally shot by the police at a high-rise building in Vaughan, Ontario, had been in a long-running feud with the condo board.

2022-12-19.title: Putin’s visit signals pressure is mounting on Belarus’s leader to expand support for Russia’s war.
2022-12-19.abstract: WARSAW — Under mounting Kremlin pressure to provide more support for Russia’s war in Ukraine, President Aleksandr G. Lukashenko of Belarus is hosting a rare visit by his Russian counterpart, Vladimir V. Putin, on Monday.

2022-12-20.title: King Charles III Is the New Face of U.K. Money
2022-12-20.abstract: Pound notes with the new sovereign’s image will begin appearing in 2024, though money with his mother’s likeness will still be in circulation.

2022-12-20.title: Surrender to a Drone? Ukraine Is Urging Russian Soldiers to Do Just That.
2022-12-20.abstract: Capitalizing on reports of low Russian morale, Ukraine has begun offering enemy troops detailed instructions on how to make their way over the battlefield and lay down their arms.

2022-12-20.title: One Day With an Ambulance in Britain: Long Waits, Rising Frustration
2022-12-20.abstract: Britain is experiencing a crisis in ambulance response times, part of a broader breakdown in the country’s revered National Health Service. We rode with one crew to witness the problems in real time.

2022-12-20.title: As Tunisia Drifts Farther From Democracy, Voters Shun Election
2022-12-20.abstract: Turnout in Saturday’s parliamentary elections was just over 11 percent, reflecting deep skepticism that politics can solve the North African nation’s grave governmental and economic crises.

2022-12-20.title: 12 Hours in a U.K. Ambulance
2022-12-20.abstract: Britain is experiencing an ambulance delay crisis. We saw the challenges during a 12-hour shift with paramedics from north Wales.

2022-12-21.title: Gunman in Canada Condo Killings Was Being Forced Out of Building
2022-12-21.abstract: The police on Tuesday identified the five people killed in a shooting one day before the gunman was scheduled to appear in court on orders to sell his unit.

2022-12-21.title: Dark Christmas Signals Tough Times for a Prosperous City in Hungary
2022-12-21.abstract: Dimmed festive lighting at an annual market in the city of Gyor underscores the economic pain being felt across Hungary as energy costs and inflation soar.

2022-12-21.title: A Putin ally meets with China’s top leader, highlighting ties as Russia’s isolation grows.
2022-12-21.abstract: China has remained a significant, if largely silent, counterweight to the international censure of Russia.

2022-12-21.title: U.K. Ambulance Workers Stage Their Largest Walkout in Decades
2022-12-21.abstract: The industrial action, seeking higher pay and better working conditions, was driven by a cost-of-living crisis and coincides with strikes by rail workers, nurses and others.

2022-12-21.title: Putin admits shortcomings but vows ‘no limits’ to Russia’s war spending.
2022-12-21.abstract: The Russian leader’s speech to defense officials was his latest effort to show he has no intention of giving up in the war on Ukraine.

2022-12-22.title: Their Final Moments: Victims of a Russian Atrocity in Bucha
2022-12-22.abstract: Through text messages, death certificates, social media posts, satellite imagery and personal testimony, we retraced the final moments of dozens of Ukrainians killed along one street in Bucha.

2022-12-22.title: The Ukrainian leader sought to convey the reality of the war to Washington.
2022-12-22.abstract: In his dramatic, daylong visit to Washington, President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine sought to bring the sounds and smells of the war’s front lines to the decision makers in White House and the Congress, who he said had the ability to speed up Ukraine’s victory and bring the war to an end.

2022-12-22.title: Caught on Camera, Traced by Phone: The Russian Military Unit That Killed Dozens in Bucha
2022-12-22.abstract: Exclusive phone records, documents, interviews and thousands of hours of video reveal how a Russian paratrooper unit killed dozens of people on one street in March.

2022-12-22.title: Nepali Court Orders Release of French Killer From Prison
2022-12-22.abstract: The action trims the life sentence Charles Sobhraj, known as ‘the Serpent,’ who targeted Western tourists in Asia in the 1970s and ’80s.

2022-12-22.title: To Save a Ruin, Send in the Sheep
2022-12-22.abstract: The archaeological park of Pompeii has found a low-tech way to prevent the site from being overrun by vegetation: hungry sheep.

2022-12-23.title: The Senate passes a spending bill that includes nearly $50 billion in aid for Ukraine.
2022-12-23.abstract: WASHINGTON — The Senate on Thursday approved a roughly $1.7 trillion spending package that would fund the federal government into next fall and send another round of financial assistance to Kyiv, a day after lawmakers welcomed President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine to Capitol Hill.

2022-12-23.title: His Job Description? Protecting German Democracy. Literally.
2022-12-23.abstract: Thomas Haldenwang runs Germany’s Office for the Protection of the Constitution. His mission is to fight the enemies of German democracy — from far-right coup plotters to Russian hackers.

2022-12-23.title: At German Christmas Markets, Smiles Shine Bright but Budgets Are Tight
2022-12-23.abstract: In Nuremberg, the stalls are open without Covid restrictions, and big crowds are returning to sip mulled wine and socialize. But amid economic uncertainty, visitors are spending less.

2022-12-23.title: As Cases Explode, China’s Low Covid Death Toll Convinces No One
2022-12-23.abstract: China’s murky statistics are fueling widespread public distrust. Its narrow definition of Covid deaths “will very much underestimate the true death toll,” the W.H.O. says.

2022-12-23.title: Iran condemns Zelensky’s remarks to Congress as ‘baseless.’
2022-12-23.abstract: Iran has condemned President Volodymyr Zelensky’s remarks to the U.S. Congress, warning the Ukrainian leader against further accusing Tehran of supplying weapons to Russia for use in the war.

2022-12-24.title: A Christmas Tree Brings Life to a Destroyed Palestinian Village
2022-12-24.abstract: For former residents of Iqrit, where Israeli forces leveled everything but the church in 1951, religious rituals bind them to a place from which they were expelled.

2022-12-24.title: Lonely, Single and Over 50? China Has a Game Show for That.
2022-12-24.abstract: Several popular television programs in the country are helping to encourage conversations about the social, romantic and sexual needs of older Chinese people.

2022-12-24.title: Putin Wants Fealty, and He’s Found It in Africa
2022-12-24.abstract: As Moscow wages war in Ukraine, its mercenaries have already established control in the Central African Republic — with scant Western reaction.

2022-12-24.title: At Least 10 Killed in Kherson Shelling, Fueling Anger Over Christmas Strikes
2022-12-24.abstract: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine had urged vigilance after the military noted the presence of Russian ships carrying cruise missiles in the Black Sea.

2022-12-24.title: The Syrian Family That Rebuilt a Chocolate Empire in Nova Scotia
2022-12-24.abstract: Seven years on, Peace by Chocolate continues to expand, and its founder has become an advocate of more open immigration.

2022-12-25.title: In Ukraine, Christmas Lights Defy Darkness of War, and Children Ask for Peace
2022-12-25.abstract: One town is protecting a statue of St. Nick with sandbags, another is putting its Christmas tree underground and others are cranking up noisy generators to keep the holiday spirit alive.

2022-12-25.title: Putin Insists He Is Open to Talks, as Air-Raid Sirens Sound Across Ukraine
2022-12-25.abstract: Ukrainians appeared intent on celebrating Christmas, even if scarce power left some cities darkened, save for generator-powered holiday light displays.

2022-12-25.title: Another Day, Another Crash: Life by Britain’s Most Bashed Bridge
2022-12-25.abstract: A railway bridge with a shallow underpass is bashed into by vehicles with startling regularity, causing mostly embarrassment, but sending cargo — flowers, bicycles, eggs and potatoes — flying onto the roadway.

2022-12-25.title: How Citizen Spies Foiled Putin’s Grand Plan for One Ukrainian City
2022-12-25.abstract: A partisan cell in Kherson spied on, undermined and even hunted down Russian soldiers. Now that Vladimir V. Putin’s forces are gone, people feel free to talk — and to brag a little.

2022-12-25.title: U.N. Fears 180 Rohingya Refugees in Stranded Boat Are Dead
2022-12-25.abstract: Families lost contact weeks ago with their relatives on the boat, the U.N. refugee agency says, while at least some of those on another boat adrift for weeks appear to have been rescued.

2022-12-26.title: In Ukraine, Christmas lights defy the darkness of war.
2022-12-26.abstract: KYIV, Ukraine — After hundreds of missiles and drones aimed at Ukraine’s energy infrastructure left dozens of towns without Christmas lights, some cities found inventive ways to win back the holiday season with decorations — and without wasting precious electricity.

2022-12-26.title: Ukraine is offering Russian soldiers detailed instructions on how to surrender to its drones.
2022-12-26.abstract: KYIV, Ukraine — Tens of thousands of drones have been employed across Ukraine to kill the enemy, spy on its formations and guide bombs to their targets. But this month the Ukrainian military began a program to use drones in a more unusual role: to guide Russian soldiers who want to surrender.

2022-12-26.title: North Korean Drones Breach Border, Triggering Alarms in South
2022-12-26.abstract: South Korean war planes fired warning shots, while the authorities briefly prohibited commercial planes from taking off at two international airports near Seoul.

2022-12-26.title: With Record Military Incursions, China Warns Taiwan and the U.S.
2022-12-26.abstract: Taiwan said China sent 71 military aircraft near the island days after President Biden bolstered U.S. support for Taiwan.

2022-12-26.title: In Nord Stream Mystery, Baltic Seabed Provides a Nearly Ideal Crime Scene
2022-12-26.abstract: As investigators piece together clues, Russia has quietly taken steps to begin expensive repairs on the giant gas pipeline, complicating theories about who was behind September’s sabotage.

2022-12-27.title: Russian City Mourns Its Lost Soldiers, but Doesn’t Resent Putin’s War
2022-12-27.abstract: Ryazan, not far from Moscow, regularly sends young men off to fight. Some are now buried in the local cemetery, but there is no surge of outrage against Russian leaders.

2022-12-27.title: In Record Numbers, an Unexpected Migrant Group Is Fleeing to the U.S.
2022-12-27.abstract: Hundreds of thousands of Nicaraguans have fled their country in recent years, escaping poverty and repression under an increasingly authoritarian government.

2022-12-27.title: ‘Tragic Battle’: On the Front Lines of China’s Covid Crisis
2022-12-27.abstract: Medical staff are outnumbered and working sick as the nation’s health care system buckles under the strain of a spiraling crisis.

2022-12-27.title: Navalny accuses the prison authorities of using his health as a tool to put pressure on him.
2022-12-27.abstract: The jailed Russian opposition leader said that he had been injected with unknown medication and accused the authorities of withholding his medical records.

2022-12-27.title: Lee Myung-bak, South Korean Ex-President, Receives Pardon
2022-12-27.abstract: The action by the current president, Yoon Suk Yeol, will release Mr. Lee from a 17-year sentence for bribery and embezzlement and nullify the enormous fines he owed.

2022-12-28.title: Wild and Wilde: At Celebrity Cemetery, Nature Takes on Starring Role
2022-12-28.abstract: Père-Lachaise in Paris, whose tombs of Oscar Wilde, Jim Morrison, Édith Piaf and many other artistic icons have made it a popular tourist draw, has become a haven for wildlife, too.

2022-12-28.title: As Remote Workers Flock to Mexico City, Airbnb and Housing Prices Soar
2022-12-28.abstract: American and Europeans are using Airbnb to find long-term rentals in Mexico’s capital, pushing housing costs higher and, critics say, forcing out local residents.

2022-12-28.title: South Koreans’ Steely Nerves Are Shaken by North Korean Drones
2022-12-28.abstract: With tensions on the Korean Peninsula on the rise, many in the South were rattled when five drones from the North successfully crossed the border without being shot down.

2022-12-28.title: China’s New Covid Chapter
2022-12-28.abstract: The end of the country’s restrictions has exposed two major vulnerabilities.

2022-12-28.title: Former Pope Benedict XVI Is ‘Very Ill,’ Francis Says
2022-12-28.abstract: Francis called on the faithful to offer up support for his 95-year-old predecessor as the Vatican said that the retired pope’s health had deteriorated in recent hours.

2022-12-29.title: Ukraine gives an optimistic update on its power grid, but concern grows about coming holidays.
2022-12-29.abstract: Ukrainian officials are optimistic that rolling power outages will not be necessary around the New Year holidays as crews work to repair the country’s power grid, which has been battered by Russian strikes.

2022-12-29.title: Fire at Cambodian Casino Near Thai Border Kills at Least 19
2022-12-29.abstract: The blaze at Grand Diamond City also injured at least 60 people, officials said. Guests at the 17-story complex jumped from windows to escape the flames.

2022-12-29.title: The Biden administration tries to stop Iran from supplying Russia with drones.
2022-12-29.abstract: WASHINGTON — The Biden administration has launched a broad effort to halt Iran’s ability to produce and deliver drones to Russia for use in the war in Ukraine, an endeavor that has echoes of its yearslong program to cut off Tehran’s access to nuclear technology.

2022-12-29.title: Moscow on the Med: A Faraway War Transforms a Turkish Resort Town
2022-12-29.abstract: Tens of thousands of Russians and Ukrainians have settled in Antalya, on Turkey’s southern coast, hoping to avoid the fallout of Russia’s war in Ukraine and start new lives.

2022-12-29.title: China Responds to U.S. Covid Testing Rule With a Collective Shrug
2022-12-29.abstract: The U.S. travel rule drew a muted response in a country grappling with outbreaks and where mass, regular Covid testing was up until recently a daily reality for many millions of people.

2022-12-30.title: Myanmar’s Ousted Leader Gets 33 Years in Prison, a Likely Life Sentence
2022-12-30.abstract: Daw Aung San Suu Kyi has faced a series of charges since being detained in a coup in early 2021. Her trials came to an end on Friday, capping months of legal proceedings.

2022-12-30.title: 100 Years Since the Birth of the Soviet Union, in Pictures
2022-12-30.abstract: It is the disintegration of the U.S.S.R. that President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia says he wants to remedy by waging war against Ukraine; it is the legacy of Moscow’s dominance that Ukrainians hope to free themselves of by defeating Moscow.

2022-12-30.title: Welcome to Little Kiwi, N.Y.C.
2022-12-30.abstract: Young New Zealanders are once again venturing back out into the world.

2022-12-30.title: A Cop Called Coco, an Actor Named Mani, a Quebecer Exploring Quebec
2022-12-30.abstract: When Mani Soleymanlou began acting, he was offered roles as stereotypical outsiders. That he now stars as a cop named Coco is indicative of broader shifts in a changing Quebec.

2022-12-30.title: In the Pacific, Outcry Over Japan’s Plan to Release Fukushima Wastewater
2022-12-30.abstract: The proposal has angered many of Japan’s neighbors, particularly those with the most direct experience of unexpected exposure to dangerous levels of radiation.

2022-12-31.title: Putin and Zelensky will give dueling addresses today with no end to fighting in sight.
2022-12-31.abstract: Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine and Vladimir V. Putin of Russia will share their New Year’s messages as the war grinds on toward its one-year anniversary.

2022-12-31.title: Benedict XVI, First Modern Pope to Resign, Dies at 95
2022-12-31.abstract: He defined a conservative course for the Roman Catholic Church, but his papacy was noted for his struggle with the clergy sexual abuse scandal and for his unexpected resignation.

2022-12-31.title: For Palestinians, a Rush to Claim ‘Martyrs’ Killed by Israel
2022-12-31.abstract: In one of the deadliest years for Palestinians, a tradition of political and armed groups claiming Palestinian “martyrs” clouds the distinction between civilian and fighter.

2022-12-31.title: Clergymen or Spies? Churches Become Tools of War in Ukraine
2022-12-31.abstract: Ukrainian officials are cracking down on a branch of the Russian Orthodox Church that they describe as a subversive force doing the Kremlin’s bidding.

2022-12-31.title: Victor Hugo Statue Takes a Prominent Place in France’s Debate on Race
2022-12-31.abstract: After a restoration darkened the hue of a statue at the birthplace of the French writer, complaints ensued — then vandalism.
